title: Portal/public integration impact assessment

claimsAuth.currentBehavior: Reads custom token claims (role, customerRef) and signs out users without expected claims.

currentImplementation: Realtime request feed via requests onSnapshot filtered by customer.id and ordered by status/requestStartDate.
Request state transitions persisted by direct document set in requests/{docId}.
Contractor assignment uses customerLocationContractors read + requestContractorPriority write.
migrationRisks: Loss of realtime parity if API replaces onSnapshot with polling only.
State-transition integrity risk if business rules move client-side to server-side without explicit transition contracts.
Contractor prioritization side effects need atomic update behavior.

currentImplementation: Uses visibility levels public/internal/private on log entries.
Sets new-notes flags on request documents (e.g., newNotesHq) to manage unread state.
migrationRisks: History ordering and timestamp shape must remain stable for existing UI render logic.
Visibility enforcement currently implied by client conventions; must become server-authoritative.
Unread-note markers can desync without event-driven update semantics.

currentImplementation: No standalone notifications collection in portal client; notifications are implicit through request status and note flags.
UI text and workflows assume downstream notification dispatch on status transitions (assign contractor, request more information, accept/cancel).
Dashboard and request views rely on near-realtime updates to represent new work.
migrationRisks: If notification fan-out is not triggered by API transition endpoints, managers/contractors may miss events.
Delayed delivery without websocket/SSE replacement degrades operational responsiveness.
apiNeeds: Server-side event dispatch bound to request transition endpoints
Optional GET /api/portal/notifications or SSE channel for unread/event stream parity

webPublicMaster.assessment: Low migration impact for backend integration because repository is static (HTML/CSS/vendor assets) with no app-layer Firebase calls.
webPublicMaster.recommendation: Treat as branding/content surface; only update links/session entrypoints if portal auth routes change.

phases: Introduce typed portal API client and replace Firebase wrapper methods behind compatibility adapters.
Migrate request list/detail and status update endpoints first; keep old Firestore listeners behind feature flag for rollback.
Migrate history/notes endpoints with visibility and unread-marker parity checks.
Enable server-side notification dispatch, then remove Firebase-specific request/note flag writes.
parityChecks: Status transitions preserve existing allowed-state matrix.
History items maintain descending chronology and visibility filtering.
Unread/new-note indicators update within equivalent latency budget.
Dashboard counts remain consistent for selected date ranges.

acceptanceCriteria: Request, note, and notification flows are fully mapped from Firebase operations to platform API responsibilities.
Critical migration risks are identified with concrete mitigations and API requirements.
Portal/public split impact is documented, including low backend impact for web-public static site.
